seoTitle: Mount Tomah vs Mount Victoria — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Tomah and Mount Victoria. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Tomah vs Mount Victoria: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Tomah and Mount Victoria.
heading: Mount Tomah vs Mount Victoria — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Tomah, New South Wales and Mount Victoria, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Victoria has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Mount Tomah (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Tomah or Mount Victoria safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Tomah has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Mount Victoria scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Tomah scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Victoria scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Tomah or Mount Victoria?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Victoria has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
